name: Khu du lịch sinh thái Thung Nham - Ninh Bình

reviews.author: Ethan Gertzman
reviews.text: Really really beautiful way to spend the afternoon. 150k to enter , includes two boat rides, one to caves and one to see the bird sanctuary. Really fun. Tranquil nature trails and beautiful flower gardens. Parts of the place were full of crowded busses full of Vietnamese kids, a little rowdy. But as you made your way through it got more and more tranquil. Highly recommend. Pretty around sunset.

reviews.author: Reinhold Braun
reviews.text: Updated and corrected: It's okay. 5/10. Nice views. Tourist prices, but that was to be expected. The entrance fee is 150k (it was increased by 50% from last years price. The boat ride to the buddha cave that is located close to the betrance is apparently free (I was just spooked by the entrance fee sign that was not clear if I need or don't need to pay. Would be nice if the entrance lady could mention it or put it on the physical map that you give out to the visitors. I am unaware if the boat to the Bird Valley is extra or not. Mermaid cave was greate and the viewpoint of the bird valley as well as the flower gardens were stunning. You can spend here easily 2-4 hours.

reviews.author: Anand Jain
reviews.text: Amazing views, boat rides and caves you can get lost in. Perfect place where you can get to see hundreds of migratory birds

reviews.author: Bhav Virdi
reviews.text: We picked our bikes and went to the Bird Sanctuary, it’s was 9 km ride from our home stay, and we were exhausted when we reached there. However the place is magical and very unique. Its peaceful and very well maintained. Lots of signage and directions available. We trekked up to the high point and then saw the birds nesting. You can also visit the 2 caves here: Buddha Cave and the Mermaid Cave. Boat rides are included in the ticket. Tickets cost Viet đ150k/ person. It was a long day but worth the 4-5 hours of your day we spent there. 😊

reviews.author: Ewa Tomczyk
reviews.text: Only now I have actually seen the reviews, and I really don't understand what it is all about ? My visit to the park was nothing but extraordinary. And yes, I paid the same entrance fees, yet I didn't take the boat. Instead, I cycled every possible path around the park. Of course, there is a resort and work ongoing around the resort, but I went there for the park, and the park didn't dossapoint. In fact, it was absolutely amazing that I would pay again to get to the top viewing point where I sat alone in peace and quiet on an easy morning. I couldn't believe how such an amazing place ( with lots of birds around) was so quiet. There was literally no one but me giving the special feeling of the experience. On my way back, I did see a few other people with bicycles, but overall, all around the park, which doesn't stop on the resort and the bird point, I had fun and I would highly recommend coming in the morning. I had my private slice of paradise.

reviews.author: Holly Kirkham
reviews.text: The bird sanctuary park was amazing definitely worth paying the 150k to get into the park. Boat trips included in the ticket price. Visit caves, flower gardens, temples and more. Brilliant day out, worth hiring bicycles and cycling around as it’s very far to walk the whole thing and scooters aren’t allowed. One of the best experiences in Tam Coc. Go!!

reviews.author: Jesse Bowskill
reviews.text: Pretty cool, boat tours are included in the ticket and tip may be asked but is not necessary. There were plenty of birds inside the “bird valley” area despite being not migration season. Your driver might offer to buy the tickets for you, as long as you check it’s the same price this should be fine. They get commission for bringing people there and getting tickets for them so you’re just helping them out a little.

reviews.author: ofir traitel
reviews.text: Beautiful place, really great care for the environment and nature there. Great views, birds and some caves. 2 cruises included in a 150k price tag for entry. If you ok with a bit of an accent tge first cave on the right worth the view from the top. I would recommend coming with a bicycle or motorbike but a walk is more than nice to take a slower approach.

reviews.author: Thuha Ng
reviews.text: One of the best place that we have had here. Yes, we will certainly come back again. The entrance ticket is vnd 150k for an adult, and vnd 40k for eletric vehicle to go back and forth. It is totally worthy traveling this amazing natural area. We spent almost a day to visit Buddha Cave, Flowers Parade, and Bird Island, try local foods (jack fruit and mango) and take pics here. Although in a summer day, it is not really hot. Bringing water and sun screen are suitable for this trip. Staffs here are quite friendly and helpful although sometimes they are a little bit talkative 🥴

reviews.author: Koos Geleijnse
reviews.text: Great value for money. Entrance is 150k and includes the boatrides and caves. The bird walk is strange however as it's a dead end and could be looped. Do go towards the end though where you can watch the birds from a viewpoint higher up. The caves, boatride, birds and flower garden make it a very worthwhile visit.

reviews.author: Arthur Lenaers
reviews.text: Boring boring boring, useless cave with some rocks that look like animals if you are on shrooms. Bird tour was more of a “look at the white Guys tour” I got more fingers pointed at me and pictures taken than the unimpressed birds. I had to wait 20min because apparently I can’t get on a boat first even when I arrived first, just because I don’t speak Vietnamese. Swing was fun though but not worth 160k (10k for parking????????????????? Just include it in the price how stupid is this). Don’t get the buggy, it’s an even bigger scam than this uninteresting boring place. Just save the time and go ANYWHERE else

reviews.author: Hajni Kosár
reviews.text: It is worth arriving individually in the morning hours to avoid the huge tourist crowds. A truly beautiful place, with wonderful flower gardens. You can easily and comfortably get around by bike.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: A lovingly designed park surrounded by stunning nature. And numerous birds that you can watch from the boat. Nice service and a more than fair entrance fee. In our opinion, definitely worth a visit! BUT we’ve been in the park from about 11 am - 2 pm. When we left it got terribly crowded. So try to visit in the morning.

reviews.author: Nate McCarthy
reviews.text: Amusement park! I was expecting a tranquil, natural setting with some wetlands and birds. Instead this felt much more like Disneyland. We did go on a holiday weekend and there were throngs of Vietnamese tourists. We got there around 4:30pm on a Saturday which was just in time apparently, since after elbowing our way thru the crowds to get on a boat, there were huge lines when we got off the boat. All that said, we did see an insane amount of birds on the boat ride, which lasted maybe 25min or so. The grounds are also very lovely and we only explored about half of the area. Really beautiful bonsai trees and landscaping. We took the electric tram out to the boat launch and walked back. If you had plenty of time and it weren’t wicked hot, it would be nice to walk the entire thing. It’s not free to go here. We paid 150k/person for entry which included the boat ride. We paid an additional 40k/person for the electric tram, which maybe wasn’t necessary but was pretty affordable in any case. We also paid 20k to park our motorbike. My suggestion: check this place out in the evening, say around 5pm, on a weekday—and definitely not on a holiday! And don’t expect a natural setting—expect boardwalks, gift shops, restaurants, all the trappings of a theme park

reviews.author: C. D.
reviews.text: This was such a lovely trip! I would say go closer to the morning time due to the heat. You can drive your scooter here or bicycle. It is very beautiful and you get access to a boat ride through a cave. There are some steep hills if you are walking or riding a bike. Cost 150k

reviews.author: Kirk Holz
reviews.text: We enjoyed our visit to this park. The boat around the bird valley and in the Buddha cave was nice, the walking the grounds was good with many displays and places for photos, and we generally had a good time here.

reviews.author: Holli Pruhsmeier
reviews.text: Loved the more natural trails around the rookery. So many species of birds out here. We walked the trail in the early morning and there was no one else here. The birds were super active. The bamboo bridges were fun to cross over along the trail getting here. I can see why some people with low mobility would take the boats to see the area since the walk was quite long.

reviews.author: Elle K
reviews.text: Amazing flower gardens within a botanical park, crane/stork rookery and and peaceful atmosphere. Allow at least two to three hours to visit. Birds come in to roost late in the day so aim for latest boat ride to see bird island. We went in winter but imagine the gardens would be more spectacular in summer.

reviews.author: Phuong Nguyen
reviews.text: We went around 9am on Thursday end of July so it wasn't too busy. The park entry fees costs 150k and includes boat rides. Because of the season the caves were not safe to enter either by foot or boat so we can't comment, but the birds sightseeing boat ride which lasts around 30mins was quite a pleasant experience. We didn't see as many birds as we were told they'd flock back around 3pm onwards but it's still cool to see different birds in their natural habitat. The ecopark however was full of butterflies and flowers which was quite pretty. When we come back next time we'd probably aim to come when it's fruit season and later in the afternoon so we can see more birds and pick fruits.

reviews.author: swati Aswal
reviews.text: Totally worth to visit if you like greenery, scenic views and birds. Though I was able to see 2 type of birds as it was the migration time. But the view and boat ride was amazing. There are caves too which are quite dark and scared me a bit but yes the history behind these tunnels were inspiring. One thing that upsets me was they had some music concert going on in open and on the other side birds breeding period was happening. That was quite distracting and disappointing at the same time. These places bird sanctuaries are places to birds, migratory birds and that need to be noise free and free from all anthropogenic activities.

reviews.author: Diane Catanzaro
reviews.text: We almost didn’t go due to some of the negative reviews. We are SO glad we went. Even if you didn’t see a bird, the landscaping, flowers, sculptures, and gardens are gorgeous. To see the birds you take a 30 min or so boat ride to the sanctuary part. We saw hundreds of birds nesting in the trees. We heard that after 4 pm is when the birds are in their nests, so we did the boat part around 4:15. Fantastic! Maybe they are also present in the morning (park opens 7:30 am), but first hand we saw many birds in late afternoon in March. Maybe the negative reviewers went in the heat of the day or a time of year when they’re not there??… I can’t say. This was the highlight of our time in this area Tam Coc. Important: separate from admission, you pay another fee to use the electric shuttles. Save that ticket! The park is many many acres and you will definitely want to use the electric shuttles. Super convenient you can even message the driver for pickup. Be sure to do the flower garden area, there are flowers all over the place, but there is a special garden area that’s off the hook and even a small windmill in a nod to the gardens near Amsterdam. This place is a beautifully manicured and well curated experience. Maybe that’s what people meant by ‘theme park’. We are two Americans and a native Vietnamese and we all absolutely loved it. The only thing I didn’t think was all that great was the mermaid cave… Not because of all the steps to get into it but because once you’re there, you have to crouch low due to the overhanging rocks. Skip that, but make this park and bird sanctuary a must-see destination unless you dislike beautiful gardens and landscaping. Oh, also, as it gets closer to dusk, the lanterns that dot the whole place get turned on, and it’s even more beautiful.

reviews.author: Walter Benjamin
reviews.text: It's a botanical garden with a carefully organised environment. Everything is neat and clean, but don't expect to see wildlife. It's a very popular place with Vietnamese tourists. The entry price is 150,000 VND plus 10,000 VND for parking. The price also includes a short boat trip to the bird habitats, where you can observe wildlife. It's a place worth visiting.

reviews.author: clemence Juin
reviews.text: Allow yourself 4 hour there, we had to do our trip in 2 hours, we did not had time to see everything at all, its beautiful, better go with your bike than motorbike.

reviews.author: Mohona Paul
reviews.text: Kudos to the way they have preserved the place for birds! They have a boat facility to take you to the birds area and you have to maintain silence to have an incredible experience. Also the boat ride to a completely dark cave is amazing. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Tony Day
reviews.text: A nice day out exploring the grounds and caves. I wouldn't put it at the top of the list for Ninh Binh, but if you have some extra time it's a nice spot to get away from all of the European tourists. The cave boat tour is pleasant as well, you can see lots of bats in there. You can drive up on a scooter and grab tickets at the gate a kilometre or so from the park on the main road. The cave just after the ticket office has around 450 steps. It might be the sweatiest I have ever been!

reviews.author: Neil Tunnacliffe
reviews.text: This place is sadly not tourist friendly A ticket to enter Bird valley is 120,000 dong. You buy your ticket at the booth which is about 1 k from the entrance. The signage is difficult to follow and understand if you are not Vietnamese. After getting your ticket you drive to the entrance and park. We paid 10,000 dong for our scooter. The entrance is in front of you and you scan in on your ticket. The ticket gives you access to two short boat rides. One through a cave and one to Bird Island. Both are pleasant experiences. You can wander through the park then and see the flowers and gardens which are pretty well kept. The single best bit about the park is hidden behind bushes and this is the swimming pool pictured below. You have to buy a ‘combi’ ticket to get access which is 100,000 ( on top of your park ticket 120,000 dong ) each. No one tells you about it and there are no signs about it. You access through a turnstile with your combo ticket. We arrived at 10 am on a friday and by 2 pm we were the only ones there ! Amazing. So bring your swimwear for a nice swim after your tour of the gardens. We found out that you cannot buy a single ticket at 100,000 dong for the pool only which will explain why no one uses it . Bizarre. But if you want to pay the combo price you can just use the pool for the day. Don’t make the mistake of thinking that you can walk straight to the pool and go through the turnstile though. You will meet a man with a flag and a whistle who waves the flag in your face blows his whistle aggressively at you and stomps his feet like a child. He is security to make sure you first go through the main entrance and walk about 500 metres to get to the turnstile you could have just walked 50 metres to. Go figure 🤷‍♂️

reviews.author: Nadya Levina
reviews.text: The place is quite beautiful and peaceful, but I don't think it's the most iconic place in Ninh Binh. Construction is still going on, but you can already enjoy a beautiful walk. However, to be honest, I didn't see many diverse birds, as it is a wild nature. On the territory, there is an opportunity to take a boat ride on the lake to the island with storks or walk through the forest along the same route. There is a small observation deck from which you can see the bird islands. Also remembered is the flower valley, where you can take beautiful photos. I also recommend visiting the valley by bike. This is a very convenient way to get around because of the large distances between objects. The locals will insist on the need to park, but I recommend not doing so. The entrance to the territory costs 150,000 dong. Overall, I recommend visiting Thung Nham Bird Valley for those who love nature and want to relax and take a walk in the fresh air.

reviews.author: Sam Millichap
reviews.text: Easy cycle ride from Tam Coc to where you buy your ticket for 150k. Then it's a bit uphill to get to the actual park area and then downhill. You can park your bicycle next to the motorbikes. I went to see the cranes who come back to the trees around 4pm onwards. The boats will take you close to them in the trees. But there was also the very long cave to walk down into and the boat ride into the cave. You could spend a lot of time here because there are gardens and flowers too and lots of beautiful places to drink a coffee. On a hot day I imagine this would be a great natural environment to relax.

reviews.author: Leong Chee Wai
reviews.text: I was expecting to see many types of birds here but we only saw mostly white egrets and some herons. The entrance ticket included 2 boat rides. 1st boat ride took us into a cave to see a rock formation that looked like a buddha and then a colony of bats. 2nd boat ride took us across the lake to see the habitat of some egrets and herons. The main attraction has to be the landscaping, stilt houses and flower orchards. For another VND40,000 you can buy a ticket for buggy. It will drop and pick you up from 6 popular drop off points.

reviews.author: Regina Eiserle
reviews.text: We arrived to this park with the expectation to see wildlife and birds. If you come for that reason, you will be disappointed. The restaurant also was terrible with the staff not speaking a single word of English and our fried rice with egg arriving without an egg, the rice tasted old and was full of oil. We left without finishing the meal. However for spending a few hours walking around it is quite nice and seems to become a theme park in the near future. Just be aware that there is a lot of construction going on and bird wise we only saw bats in the cave and storchs from a far distance.
